L1.area: Tool and integration node configuration processing layer

L2.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where user-provided server configuration input is dynamically evaluated using a JavaScript code execution mechanism instead of a safe data parser. An attacker can craft a malicious configuration string that, when submitted via the API, causes arbitrary JavaScript to execute with full Node.js runtime privileges, enabling system command execution, file system access, and data exfiltration.

L3.area: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration and connection handling nodes
L3.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where user-provided MCP server configuration input is dynamically evaluated using a JavaScript code execution mechanism instead of a safe data parser. An attacker can craft a malicious configuration string that, when submitted through the node configuration API endpoint, causes arbitrary JavaScript to execute with full Node.js runtime privileges, enabling system command execution, file system access, and data exfiltration.

ground_truth.reason: The convertToValidJSONString function uses the Function() constructor to evaluate user-provided MCP server configuration strings, allowing arbitrary JavaScript code execution with full Node.js runtime privileges.
